Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Joe - Looks good to me; will wait till cob today if Michael S or Mike H has any other comment.  Btw, the corresponding rst under doc will need to be updated also (https://git.onap.org/dcaegen2/tree/platformdoc/docs/components/dcae-cli/blueprint-generator/blueprint_generator.rst)

Patch Set 3:

I like the new implementation of the command-line interface code.  `picocli` makes the code better organized.  One question I have is whether we can still use the blueprint generator as a library (DCAE MOD has a service that needs this).  I want to make sure that the same jar can we used in that case.  Thanks

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> I like the new implementation of the command-line interface code.  `picocli` makes the code better organized.  One question I have is whether we can still use the blueprint generator as a library (DCAE MOD has a service that needs this).  I want to make sure that the same jar can we used in that case.  Thanks

Hi Michael, 

I kept all the command options the same the only change is adding the positional to indicate if its policy or blueprint.
I am not quite sure of how DCAE MOD is using the generator, but if it was running it via CLI, it might need to update the way it executes slightly. If it is using the classes directly then it depends on which classes it is using.
If it is directly using any classes other than the BlueprintGenerator or PolicyCreate classes then I guess it would not need to change. Those two classes were pretty much just main methods with some methods to support it, so I don't know how it would be using them, but if it is then it might need to change slightly to run the runnable as opposed to running the main method.

Would need more info on how it was getting to determine if it is significant changes or trivial ones

> 
> Would need more info on how it was getting to determine if it is significant changes or trivial ones

We are using BPG's classes directly.  We basically have a version of the code that's under your BlueprintCommand.run function.

I don't *think* there should be any issue but my concern is about loading the JAR specifically.  I noticed the pom.xml and how the JAR is going to get built is different now.  This shouldn't affect my app that loads the JAR and uses the underlying classes directly right?  Thanks again

The original jar is still built, the new uberjar has -executable at the end of it.
In addition, I left the tar building in, as I wasn't sure if others were using that for something.
Change has been successfully merged by Vijay Venkatesh Kumar
